headline: Nvidia’s $20 Billion Groq Deal Queried by Warren, Blumenthal
summary: Senators Elizabeth Warren from Massachusetts and Richard Blumenthal of Connecticut sent Nvidia Chief Executive Officer Jensen Huang a letter on Thursday night, asking for additional information on the deal, which they said appeared to have been structured “to evade scrutiny by antitrust regulators.”  In their letter, the senators wrote, “We are concerned that this takeover could stifle competition, further entrenching Nvidia’s dominance in the AI chip industry and ceding our technological leadership to China.”

content.title: Nvidia’s $20 Billion Groq Deal Queried by Warren, Blumenthal
content.summary: Senators Elizabeth Warren from Massachusetts and Richard Blumenthal of Connecticut sent Nvidia Chief Executive Officer Jensen Huang a letter on Thursday night, asking for additional information on the deal, which they said appeared to have been structured “to evade scrutiny by antitrust regulators.”  In their letter, the senators wrote, “We are concerned that this takeover could stifle competition, further entrenching Nvidia’s dominance in the AI chip industry and ceding our technological leadership to China.”
